heroHeadline: Min Aung Hlaing Elected President (429/584 Votes) — 100+ Civilians Killed Since Junta Parliament Convened
heroSubtitle: On April 3, Myanmar's military-dominated parliament elected junta chief Min Aung Hlaing as the country's 11th president with 429 of 584 votes, completing the junta's cosmetic 'civilianization' transition condemned by the NUG, ASEAN, and human rights groups. Inauguration is expected this week. The Irrawaddy documented that since parliament convened on March 16, at least 100 civilians have been killed in SAC airstrikes — including 7 killed at a clinic in Budalin and 27 at a market in Mandalay Region. Concurrent resistance operations intensified: the KIA captured six SAC frontline positions in Hpakant (April 2), the KNLA killed five SAC soldiers in a railway outpost raid, and PDF forces killed 17 SAC troops across Kani (Sagaing) and Natmauk (Magway) on April 5. The Arakan Army continues its Sittwe siege with fighters 2 km from the city center.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, MoeMaKa, and official NUG, KNU, and AA statements.
